title: phlox kelseyi subsp. glandulosa
intro: Phlox kelseyi subsp. glandulosa is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family, known for its fragrant, pink to purple flowers and native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phlox kelseyi subsp. glandulosa include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
